summary: Implement a nightly Azure Function (Python 3.11, Linux Consumption) that queries GPT-4o via TRAPI using managed identity to retrieve the 1985 New York Yankees roster, then persists the result to an Azure Blob Storage container. All authentication uses managed identity — no API keys. Infrastructure is managed via Bicep; CI/CD via GitHub Actions.
assumptions: TRAPI refers to the Microsoft-internal Azure OpenAI proxy that supports Azure AD / managed identity authentication — no API keys required.
The Azure Function runtime is Python 3.11 on Linux Consumption plan, consistent with existing Bicep infra in /infra.
The target blob container is 'yankees-roster' as reflected in existing infra modules.
AzureWebJobsStorage uses identity-based connection (AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName) — no storage key.
RBAC roles required: Storage Blob Data Owner on the storage account for the Function's managed identity, and Cognitive Services OpenAI User or equivalent on the TRAPI-backed Azure OpenAI resource.
GitHub Actions is the CI/CD platform; Bicep is the IaC tool.
Application Insights will be provisioned for observability.
The nightly timer cron is '0 0 2 * * *' (2 AM UTC) unless otherwise specified.
Tags on all resources: project=1985-NY-Yankees, owner=rciapala.
The spec does not specify auto-approve for the task plan, so PRJ-01 requires manual approval.
open_questions: What is the exact TRAPI endpoint and Azure AD scope required for GPT-4o access — is this the Microsoft-internal TRAPI gateway or a tenant-specific Azure OpenAI deployment?
What is the desired output format for the blob — JSON array of player objects, plain text list, or CSV?
Should the blob be overwritten nightly or versioned/appended with a date-stamped file name?
What subscription and resource group should the new resources be deployed to?
Are there network restrictions (private endpoints, VNet integration) required for Storage or TRAPI access?
What is the alerting channel for failures — email, Teams, PagerDuty?
Is there a required minimum test coverage threshold?
Should the Function retry on TRAPI failure, and if so, how many times with what backoff?

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Provision foundational infrastructure, establish managed identity trust, and deliver the three core functional requirements: fetch roster via TRAPI, write to blob, execute nightly.
entry_criteria: spec.md reviewed and task plan approved (PRJ-01 complete)
Azure subscription and resource group available
TRAPI endpoint and Azure AD scope confirmed
Repository access granted to all engineers
exit_criteria: Bicep infra deploys cleanly to target environment
Managed identity has required RBAC on Storage and TRAPI
Timer-triggered Azure Function runs end-to-end: fetches roster from GPT-4o and writes blob
All unit tests pass and results are linked in PRs
All Core Build PRs reviewed by @copilot and merged

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Establish CI/CD pipeline, runtime configuration management, and Application Insights observability to support reliable ongoing delivery and operations.
entry_criteria: All Core Build wave exit criteria met
GitHub Actions secrets/OIDC federation configured for target subscription
exit_criteria: GitHub Actions workflow deploys infra and app on push to main
Application Insights resource provisioned and Function telemetry flowing
All environment variables and app settings documented and configured
All Foundation PRs reviewed by @copilot and merged

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add retry/error handling, perform security audit, and configure alerting to ensure the solution is production-grade.
entry_criteria: All Foundation wave exit criteria met
exit_criteria: Retry logic in place for transient TRAPI and Storage failures
No API keys or secrets in source code or app settings (all managed identity)
Alert rules configured for function failures and exceptions
All Hardening PRs reviewed by @copilot and merged

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate end-to-end correctness, complete documentation, and confirm production readiness with a supervised rollout.
entry_criteria: All Hardening wave exit criteria met
exit_criteria: Integration test confirms nightly run fetches all 1985 Yankees players and blob is written correctly
Production deployment successful and nightly timer confirmed active
All Release Readiness PRs reviewed by @copilot and merged

tasks.title: Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Review this engineering task plan for completeness, feasibility, and alignment with the spec.md requirements. Confirm open questions are resolved or have acceptable assumptions. Approve the plan to unblock all downstream tasks.
acceptance_criteria: All open questions reviewed and either resolved or explicitly deferred with justification
Task plan reviewed by rciapala and at least one engineer
TRAPI endpoint, AD scope, and blob output format confirmed
Plan approval documented in GitHub issue or PR comment
risks: TRAPI access details unknown — could block APP-01 if not resolved before Core Build begins
Blob output format not specified — may require rework of APP-02 if changed later

tasks.title: Provision Azure infrastructure via Bicep
tasks.description: Author and deploy Bicep modules for the Azure Function App (Linux Consumption, Python 3.11), Azure Storage Account with 'yankees-roster' blob container, and Application Insights. Use identity-based storage connection (AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName). Tag all resources project=1985-NY-Yankees, owner=rciapala.
outputs: Deployed Function App, Storage Account, blob container, Application Insights
acceptance_criteria: Bicep deploys without errors to target resource group via `az deployment group create`
Function App exists with kind=functionapp,linux, reserved=true, linuxFxVersion=Python|3.11
Storage Account exists with 'yankees-roster' blob container
AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName app setting present — no storage connection string or key
Application Insights resource provisioned and APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING set on Function App
All resources tagged project=1985-NY-Yankees, owner=rciapala
PR opened with @copilot assigned for review and merged before proceeding
Infrastructure validation steps documented and executed: `az functionapp show`, `az storage container show`, `az monitor app-insights component show`
risks: Consumption plan cold-start latency for nightly timer — acceptable given non-interactive workload
Identity-based storage requires correct RBAC before Function can start — must be coordinated with SEC-01

tasks.title: Configure managed identity RBAC for Storage and TRAPI
tasks.description: Enable system-assigned managed identity on the Function App and assign Storage Blob Data Owner role on the storage account. Assign the appropriate Azure AD role (e.g., Cognitive Services OpenAI User) on the TRAPI/Azure OpenAI resource. No API keys or secrets — all auth via managed identity.
inputs: TRAPI resource ID and required AD role (from PRJ-01 resolution)
outputs: Managed identity configured on Function App
acceptance_criteria: Function App has system-assigned managed identity enabled
Storage Blob Data Owner role assigned to Function identity on storage account
TRAPI/OpenAI role assigned to Function identity on the AI resource
RBAC assignments codified in Bicep (not manual portal assignments)
No storage keys, OpenAI API keys, or connection strings present in app settings or code
`az role assignment list --assignee <principalId>` confirms both role assignments
PR opened with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: TRAPI role name/resource ID unknown until PRJ-01 open question resolved
Role propagation delay (~5 min) may cause initial Function run failures — document in runbook

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI client to fetch 1985 Yankees roster
tasks.description: Implement a Python module (`trapi_client.py`) that authenticates to TRAPI via `azure-identity` (DefaultAzureCredential), calls GPT-4o with a prompt to return the complete 1985 New York Yankees roster, and returns a structured list of players. No API keys — bearer token from managed identity only.
inputs: TRAPI endpoint URL and AD scope (from PRJ-01)
azure-identity, openai or httpx Python packages
acceptance_criteria: Module authenticates using DefaultAzureCredential — no hardcoded credentials or API keys
GPT-4o prompt returns a structured list of all 1985 NY Yankees players
Unit tests mock the TRAPI HTTP response and validate parsing logic
All unit tests pass: `pytest tests/test_trapi_client.py -v` — link test results in PR
Module handles HTTP errors and raises typed exceptions
PR opened with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: GPT-4o may return incomplete or inconsistent player lists — prompt engineering required
TRAPI authentication flow may differ from standard Azure OpenAI SDK — verify token scope

tasks.title: Implement Azure Blob Storage writer
tasks.description: Implement a Python module (`blob_writer.py`) that uses `azure-storage-blob` with `DefaultAzureCredential` to write the player roster to the 'yankees-roster' container. Blob naming convention: `roster.json` (overwrite) or `roster-YYYY-MM-DD.json` (versioned) — confirm with PRJ-01.
acceptance_criteria: Module authenticates using DefaultAzureCredential — no storage connection strings or keys
Roster data written to 'yankees-roster' container with correct blob name
Unit tests mock BlobServiceClient and validate upload logic
All unit tests pass: `pytest tests/test_blob_writer.py -v` — link test results in PR
Module raises typed exceptions on upload failure
PR opened with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: Blob naming convention not specified — overwrite vs. versioned impacts idempotency

tasks.title: Implement nightly Timer Trigger Azure Function
tasks.description: Implement the Azure Function entry point (`function_app.py`) with a Timer Trigger configured to run nightly (CRON: `0 0 2 * * *` UTC). The function orchestrates `trapi_client.get_roster()` and `blob_writer.write_roster()`, logs execution to Application Insights, and handles top-level exceptions.
acceptance_criteria: Timer Trigger CRON expression set to nightly schedule and confirmed correct
Function calls trapi_client and blob_writer in sequence
Structured logging with player count and blob URL emitted to Application Insights
Unit tests mock trapi_client and blob_writer and validate orchestration logic
All unit tests pass: `pytest tests/test_function_app.py -v` — link test results in PR
Function deploys to Azure and manual trigger (`az functionapp function invoke`) succeeds
PR opened with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: Cold-start on Consumption plan may cause first-run latency — not a concern for nightly batch
Uncaught exceptions will silently swallow errors if not handled at top level

tasks.title: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline
tasks.description: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs unit tests on PR, then on merge to main deploys Bicep infra and publishes the Function App using OIDC federated identity (no secrets stored in GitHub). Include environment protection rules for production.
inputs: Azure subscription federated identity / OIDC configured
outputs: OIDC federated credential on Azure AD app or managed identity
acceptance_criteria: Workflow triggers on PR (test-only) and on push to main (test + deploy)
OIDC federation used — no AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET stored in GitHub secrets
Unit tests run and must pass before deploy step executes
Bicep deployed via `az deployment group create` in workflow
Function App published via `func azure functionapp publish` or equivalent
Workflow passes end-to-end on a test push to main
PR opened with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: OIDC federation setup requires Azure AD app registration with federated credential — needs subscription owner permissions
Function publish step may require Azure Functions Core Tools installed in runner

tasks.title: Configure Application Insights and structured logging
tasks.description: Extend Bicep to provision an Application Insights workspace-based resource linked to a Log Analytics workspace. Configure APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING on the Function App. Validate telemetry (traces, exceptions, custom events) flows from the Function.
outputs: Updated /infra Bicep with Log Analytics + App Insights
acceptance_criteria: Log Analytics workspace provisioned or reused
Application Insights (workspace-based) provisioned and linked to Log Analytics
APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING app setting present on Function App
Test function invocation produces traces visible in Application Insights Live Metrics or Logs
Infrastructure validated: `az monitor app-insights component show` returns correct resource
PR opened with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: Classic Application Insights (non-workspace-based) will be retired — ensure workspace-based type is used

tasks.title: Document and configure runtime app settings
tasks.description: Define all required Azure Function App settings (TRAPI endpoint URL, storage account name, blob container name, AI resource scope) as app settings in Bicep. Document each setting in README with description and whether it is slot-safe. No secrets — only non-sensitive configuration.
outputs: App settings codified in Bicep functionapp module
acceptance_criteria: All required app settings present in Bicep and deployed to Function App
No secrets, connection strings, or API keys in app settings
README lists each setting with description, example value, and slot-safety
`az functionapp config appsettings list` confirms all settings present
PR opened with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: TRAPI endpoint URL may be environment-specific — ensure it is parameterized in Bicep

tasks.title: Add retry logic and error handling
tasks.description: Add transient-fault retry logic to `trapi_client.py` and `blob_writer.py` using exponential backoff (e.g., `tenacity` library). Ensure the Function logs structured error details and does not silently swallow failures. Implement dead-letter behavior by writing failed run metadata to a separate blob.
outputs: Updated trapi_client.py and blob_writer.py with retry decorators
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI calls retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff on transient HTTP errors (429, 503, 504)
Blob write retries up to 3 times on transient storage errors
Permanent failures raise exceptions that propagate to the Function and are logged to App Insights
Unit tests cover retry scenarios with mocked failures
All unit tests pass: `pytest -v` — link test results in PR
PR opened with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: Retry storms on TRAPI rate limiting (429) — ensure retry respects Retry-After header

tasks.title: Security audit — no API keys, least-privilege RBAC
tasks.description: Conduct a security review of the full solution: verify no API keys or secrets in code, app settings, or repo history; confirm RBAC assignments follow least privilege; validate that the Function cannot access resources beyond its required scope.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub secret scanning shows zero detected secrets in repo
`az role assignment list --assignee <principalId>` shows only required roles (no Owner or Contributor)
No API keys, passwords, or connection strings in source code, app settings, or git history
Managed identity is system-assigned (not user-assigned) and scoped to the Function App
Security audit findings documented and resolved
PR opened with @copilot assigned for review (if code changes required)
risks: Developer may have accidentally committed secrets during development — git history scan required

tasks.title: Configure alerts and health monitoring
tasks.description: Create Azure Monitor alert rules for: Function execution failures, exception rate threshold breaches, and missed nightly runs (timer trigger not fired). Configure action group for notification (email or Teams webhook). Codify alert rules in Bicep.
acceptance_criteria: Alert rule fires on Function execution failure (exit code != 0 or exception logged)
Alert rule fires if no successful run detected within 26 hours (missed nightly run)
Action group delivers notification to confirmed channel
Alert rules codified in Bicep and deployed
`az monitor metrics alert show` or `az monitor scheduled-query show` confirms rules active
Test alert triggered manually and notification received
PR opened with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: Consumption plan does not expose always-on health endpoint — monitor via App Insights execution logs only

tasks.title: Integration testing and end-to-end validation
tasks.description: Execute a full end-to-end integration test against the deployed Function: manually trigger the Timer Function, verify GPT-4o returns a plausible 1985 Yankees roster, confirm the blob is written to 'yankees-roster' container, and validate the player list for completeness (expected ~40 roster members).
inputs: Deployed Function App in target environment
acceptance_criteria: Function triggered manually via `az functionapp function invoke` or Azure Portal runs without error
Blob created in 'yankees-roster' container with valid JSON player list
Player list contains known 1985 Yankees players (e.g., Don Mattingly, Dave Winfield, Rickey Henderson)
Player count is within expected range (~25-45 players)
No exceptions or errors in Application Insights logs for the test run
Integration test results documented and linked in task
risks: GPT-4o response is non-deterministic — player completeness validation requires reference data
TRAPI rate limits may throttle repeated test runs

tasks.title: Write README and operations runbook
tasks.description: Write a README covering: project overview, architecture diagram, local development setup, deployment instructions, app settings reference, and troubleshooting. Write an operations runbook covering: how to monitor nightly runs, how to re-trigger manually, common failure modes and remediation steps.
inputs: All Core Build and Foundation tasks complete
acceptance_criteria: README covers project purpose, architecture, local setup, and deployment steps
App settings table documents all Function App settings with description and slot-safety
Runbook covers: monitoring nightly runs in App Insights, manual re-trigger steps, retry/failure handling
Architecture diagram included (even if ASCII art)
PR opened with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: Documentation may become stale if written before implementation is finalized — write after APP-03

tasks.title: Production deployment and rollout validation
tasks.description: Execute the final production deployment via the GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline. Confirm the nightly Timer Trigger is scheduled and active. Verify the first nightly run succeeds and produces a correct blob. Confirm monitoring and alerts are active.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub Actions deploy workflow runs successfully to production
Function App visible in Azure Portal with Timer Trigger active
First nightly run (or manually triggered run) completes without errors
Blob written to 'yankees-roster' container in production storage account
Application Insights shows successful execution trace
Alert rules confirmed active in Azure Monitor
Go-live sign-off from rciapala documented
risks: Production RBAC propagation delay may cause first run to fail — wait 5-10 minutes post-deployment
Nightly run cannot be validated until the next day unless manually triggered
